item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So cute so sad.It reminds me of Girls'Last Tour.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even if I weren't a sucker for sad robots, 4183-A's emotional arc would have still gotten me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, how the hell did I never comment on this? I came back to this article today and the drones (pour one out for -B) are skillfully characterized , the logs mix the right balance of humor and pathos, and I almost cried AGAIN upon reaching the ending. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this from the morning and cried. Good story. +1
Woo! We made it, baybey!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for having written this. It’s beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The bear has not responded to legal threats. It is a formidable opponent."

This SCP was well written and those drone logs were funny as hell. Props to you, sir.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was well written, but curse you for making me care about maintenance drones that don't even exist yet. Curse you.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the Wall-E vibes. Great stuff

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would that accomplish?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get it.

Why'd Dr. Lesteigh shoot himself, when he could have just saved everyone a whole lot of time by shooting the skip instead?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved this one a lot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is beautiful +1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is just genuinely charming, and that's not something I usually get from SCPs. I find most of the stuff on here interesting, but I found this emotional, and that's hard to pull off in general, let alone in this format. Excellent job.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent… but I also feel like I've seen this before, as a tale or something else? I don't know why, but this gives me a really odd sense of familiarity. Maybe I ready a way way earlier draft or something. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This pleases me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy hell, that was well written.

That "Respond now." bit really triggered something in me. A subtle development of emotion. It kind of reminded me those bits in TNG where Data does something unexpected and you're just like "…wait, was that emotion?"

+1 all over

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not crying about non-existent robots…
…y-you are…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
big sad

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
top 10 things I would take a bullet for:
1. these drones

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed an instance of "SCP-4193" to 4183, upvoted, and now I'm gonna go sulk and feel bad for these drones.

Pls fix drone B in the future or I'll be sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it sounds nice out there

item.title: cool article
item.content:encoded: I liked this article, and I think the only crit I'd have is I wished the actual scp had been more written out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In universe, the reference to 9214 is entirely arbitrary and has nothing to do with whatever SCP that designation may fall on, if there even is an SCP-9214 at that point in time. The drone just picks a designation which isn't 4183 so that it can change the containment procedures of that SCP to include fighting off bears. Kind of a roundabout solution but it's drone logic, I guess. Out of universe, the use of a nonexistent SCP number avoids crosslink confusion (like if the drone said SCP-173 everyone would be wondering what it has to do with 173 when the reason it's brought up has nothing to do with any SCP at all).

Also, in universe it wouldn't make sense for anything in this article to be blackboxed. It'd have to be a drone doing it, and it doesn't seem like they've figured out how to do that kind of editing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love my drone babies….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very charming. My only criticism would be that it's bait for "not a real scp!!" complaints, which I don't quite agree with, but have a similar issue in that the SCP object appears pointless. If there was some callback to it in the documents that would be better, but as it stands there is a nice story here with some irrelevant fluff at the start. It's clearly intentionally brief so it can be got out of the way and the real story can begin, but I don't think that's as elegant as it could be.

Overall, still good stuff and easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved it! It was a really interesting perspective, hope to see more of this in the future, maybe as a tale.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Equal parts intriguing and creepy. Very well done.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I generally dislike when non-existing SCP's (SCP-9214) are used in articles, as it only really adds confusion and is about as functional as a blackbox.

However, the robots were very neat. It has a very nice ending, and the logs were short and interesting. I enjoyed reading through the majority of it (admittedly, other than the second instance of the poem about oranges), so this deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made some adjustments based on fantastic feedback from TheGreatHippo. Nothing too drastic, mainly just tightening things up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice, another Tanhony SCP!

Also, does it affect those who are blind?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
page needs tags, i'll leave that to someone with more expertise.

this feels less like a main series SCP and more like a tale with SCP containment flavor text. but it definitely feels like it's part of something bigger (we still don't know what the indigo event is!) and i love the interactions between the robots. going to give this a +1 for visibility's sake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gave crit on this, and I'm going to echo what I said on Discord.

The drones are masterfully made to have just enough character to be actual characters while not being so developed or personable that they break the veil of just being robots. Their dialogue is fitting, their decisions are understandable, and I just love them. I love them so much.

+1 for the cute lil' containment bots

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this one. It had a bit of a Wall-E, Three Robots sort of vibe. Robots carrying on after humanity has left the planet, one way or another. The dialogue is clever and charming, and I found myself engaged by characters that likely have no face, and look as divergent as their designations.

Well done! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beep boop i love 3 drones

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Wolfsblood, Mr. College, DrAstari, SirPupienus, Crashington and Laneous for their feedback!

PLEASE DON’T SUE TANHONY

https://search.creativecommons.org/photos/5c4a904f-9aed-465b-84d6-5c504be1536b (CC BY-SA 2.0)

Description of an orange is from Wikipedia.
